# JWST-Specific Search Components

This page describes elements of the search form which are either unique to JWST, or are shared with other missions but contain options that are best explained on a per-mission basis.

Duplication Checking

Do not attempt to check for duplicate observations using the new JWST search form; planned but unexecuted observations will not be included. You should either use the [MAST Portal](https://mast.stsci.edu) or follow the instructions in the [JWST Duplication Checking Notebook](https://spacetelescope.github.io/mast_notebooks/notebooks/JWST/duplication_checking/duplication_checking.html).

  


## Dataset ID

title: JWST Search Components
text:
## Dataset ID

A **Dataset ID** marks a "top-level" collection of files from an Observation. The naming convention follows a truncated version of the full [JWST file naming scheme](https://jwst-pipeline.readthedocs.io/en/latest/jwst/data_products/file_naming.html); each filename in the dataset will begin with the dataset ID, followed by indicators for the detector used and product type.

* * *

## 

## Data Type

The **Data Type** refers to the semantic content of the highest-level [pipeline product](https://jwst-docs.stsci.edu/accessing-jwst-data/jwst-science-data-overview) for the type of exposure that produced the data. Note that slitless and IFU spectral modes ultimately produce data of type _Spectrum_.

_Other_ data includes internal calibration and guide-star data.

* * *

* * *

## 

## Data Type

The **Data Type** refers to the semantic content of the highest-level [pipeline product](https://jwst-docs.stsci.edu/accessing-jwst-data/jwst-science-data-overview) for the type of exposure that produced the data. Note that slitless and IFU spectral modes ultimately produce data of type _Spectrum_.

_Other_ data includes internal calibration and guide-star data.

* * *

title: JWST Search Components
text:
_Other_ data includes internal calibration and guide-star data.

* * *

## Instrument Selector

By default, all **Instruments** are selected. The upper right of the field presents a _Select all_ vs.  _Deselect all_ choice. You can also select individual fields.

* * *

## Moving Target Flag

Enabling the **Moving Targets** flag will only return targets without fixed coordinates (i.e. solar system targets).

* * *

## Observation Mode

The **Observation Mode** selector is empty by default. Note that searching for "all" and "none" are equivalent, as all observing modes will be included in both cases. This mode does not include type-ahead, so you must select your choice from the drop-down menu.

* * *

## Observation and visit Numbers

## Observation and visit Numbers

title: JWST Search Components
text:
* * *

## Observation and visit Numbers

The **Observation Number** and **Visit Number** correspond to planning information from the submitted [APT file](https://www.stsci.edu/scientific-community/software/astronomers-proposal-tool-apt). You can find the APT file, as well as a PDF summary, by searching for the corresponding proposal on the [JWST Program Information search form](https://www.stsci.edu/jwst/science-execution/program-information).

* * *

## Proposal Cycle

  


**Proposal Cycle** is the number of the JWST Proposal Cycle in which the program was submitted and approved. This does not necessarily correspond to the epoch of the [scheduled observation](https://www.stsci.edu/jwst/science-execution/observing-schedules).

* * *

## Proposal Type

## Proposal Type

title: JWST Search Components
text:
* * *

## Proposal Type

**Proposal Type** is the programmatic category assigned to the proposal. There are several [Categories of JWST Science Observations](https://www.stsci.edu/jwst/science-execution/approved-programs). 

* * *

title: CLASSY Search Components
text:
##  Parameter Constraint Syntax

Learn how to specify constraints on search parameters depending on the nature of the input. The following specifications apply for the core search parameters and [Additional Search Parameters](/spaces/MASTDOCS/pages/104958265/Additional+Search+Parameters) in the search form.

Hovering over each filter box shows tooltips on how to use search conditions.

* * *

## CLASSY Search Parameter Components

* * *

### Redshift

The **'Redshift'** component, a [Numeric field](/spaces/MASTDOCS/pages/104958264/Search+Parameter+Overview#SearchParameterOverview-param_numerics), allows querying on redshift from optical emission lines. Type a number, conditional operation, or a range.

### Distance

title: CLASSY Search Components
text:
### Distance

The **'Distance'** component, a [Numeric field](/spaces/MASTDOCS/pages/104958264/Search+Parameter+Overview#SearchParameterOverview-param_numerics), allows querying based on distance, in Mpc. Type a number, conditional operation, or a range.

### Total Galaxy Stellar Mass

The **'Total Galaxy Stellar Mass'** component, a [Numeric field](/spaces/MASTDOCS/pages/104958264/Search+Parameter+Overview#SearchParameterOverview-param_numerics), allows querying based on total galaxy stellar mass, in M☉. Type a number, conditional operation, or a range.

### Total Galaxy Star Formation

title: CLASSY Search Components
text:
### Nebular Oxygen Abundance

The **'Nebular Oxygen Abundance'** component, a [Numeric field](/spaces/MASTDOCS/pages/104958264/Search+Parameter+Overview#SearchParameterOverview-param_numerics), allows querying based on nebular oxygen abundance, in Z☉ or 12+Log(O/H). Type a number, conditional operation, or a range.

  


Use the 'Unit' dropdown to choose the unit to query against.

### Nebular Electron Density

The **'Nebular Electron Density'** component, a [Numeric field](/spaces/MASTDOCS/pages/104958264/Search+Parameter+Overview#SearchParameterOverview-param_numerics), allows querying based on nebular electron density, in cm-3. Type a number, conditional operation, or a range.

### Ionization Field Strength

title: ULLYSES Search Components
text:
**On this page...**

# Component Overview

The **'****ULLYSES Search Parameter Components'** section contains the search parameters for the ULLYSES HLSP data collection. This page overviews the types of search components and how to specify parameter constraints.

Filtering data will return targets that include the selected criteria but are not limited to those criteria. For example, filtering by 'Telescope=FUSE' will return targets that include FUSE data _and_ data from other telescopes.

##  Parameter Constraint Syntax

title: ULLYSES Search Components
text:

### Telescope

 By default, all **'Telescope'** options are selected.  Toggle the checkboxes to select and/or deselect the Telescope selections.

### HST Instruments

 

By default, all **'HST Instrument'** options are selected.  Toggle the checkboxes to select and/or deselect the Telescope selections.

title: ULLYSES Search Components
text:

### Target Type

 

By default, all **'Target Type'** options are selected.  Toggle the check boxes to select and/or deselect the Target Type selections.

### HLSP Type

 

By default, all **'HLSP Type'** options are selected.  Toggle the checkboxes to select and/or deselect the HLSP Type selections.

### Spectral Band

 

By default, all **'Wavelength'** options are selected.  Toggle the checkboxes to select and/or deselect the Wavelength selections.

# Science Product Core Keywords

The keywords listed below are required or recommended in all FITS files (and in some cases, all FITS extensions) of any type that contain images, spectra, light curves, and other similar science products. Recommended keywords, if absent, will be derived and inserted prior to ingest.

# Science Product Core Keywords

The keywords listed below are required or recommended in all FITS files (and in some cases, all FITS extensions) of any type that contain images, spectra, light curves, and other similar science products. Recommended keywords, if absent, will be derived and inserted prior to ingest.

**HLSP Metadata Color Codes**

The following table(s) of HLSP metadata, to be included in science products, are color-coded:

title: Common Metadata
text:
The following table(s) of HLSP metadata, to be included in science products, are color-coded:

  
| Required  
---|---  
  
| Recommended  
  
| Suggested  
  
The extension column refers to the primary (P) header or, for multi-extension (MEF) files, one or more extensions (E). Depending upon the organization of the data (e.g., if image data arrays appear in an extension), any given Primary keyword may instead belong in an Extension.
text_with_context:
[Common Metadata]

The following table(s) of HLSP metadata, to be included in science products, are color-coded:

  
| Required  
---|---  
  
| Recommended  
  
| Suggested  
  
The extension column refers to the primary (P) header or, for multi-extension (MEF) files, one or more extensions (E). Depending upon the organization of the data (e.g., if image data arrays appear in an extension), any given Primary keyword may instead belong in an Extension.

title: Common Metadata
text:
| Keyword| HDU| Notes  
---|---|---|---  
  
| `DATE-BEG`| P or E| [ISO-8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) formatted DateTime string for start of an observation (or, the start of the first observation of a combined product), in the time system specified by `TIMESYS`. May use `MJD-BEG` in addition or instead. For catalogs, this time should reflect the time of the earliest observation of the entries.  
  
| `DATE-END`| P or E| ISO-8601 formatted DateTime for end of an observation (or, the end of the last observation of a combined product), in the time system specified by `TIMESYS`. May use `MJD-END `in addition or instead. For catalogs, this time should reflect the time of the latest observation of the entries.
text_with_context:
[Common Metadata]

| Keyword| HDU| Notes  
---|---|---|---  
  
| `DATE-BEG`| P or E| [ISO-8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) formatted DateTime string for start of an observation (or, the start of the first observation of a combined product), in the time system specified by `TIMESYS`. May use `MJD-BEG` in addition or instead. For catalogs, this time should reflect the time of the earliest observation of the entries.  
  
| `DATE-END`| P or E| ISO-8601 formatted DateTime for end of an observation (or, the end of the last observation of a combined product), in the time system specified by `TIMESYS`. May use `MJD-END `in addition or instead. For catalogs, this time should reflect the time of the latest observation of the entries.

## Data Simulations

title: Common Metadata
text:
Generally, data simulations should include the same metadata that apply to the data product that is being simulated. However, it is essential that users of the simulations (including software applications) be able to distinguish between simulated and observed data. Therefore all simulated data must include the following keyword in the primary header (PHDU) in FITS files:

  * `SIMULATD= T`



## Special Considerations for FITS Keywords
text_with_context:
[Common Metadata]

Generally, data simulations should include the same metadata that apply to the data product that is being simulated. However, it is essential that users of the simulations (including software applications) be able to distinguish between simulated and observed data. Therefore all simulated data must include the following keyword in the primary header (PHDU) in FITS files:

  * `SIMULATD= T`



## Special Considerations for FITS Keywords

title: Common Metadata
text:
* Note that the lists in this chapter do not include the [basic structural FITS keywords](https://www.aanda.org/index.php?option=com_article&access=standard&Itemid=129&url=/articles/aa/full_html/2010/16/aa15362-10/T30.html) (`SIMPLE`, `NAXIS`, etc.) that are required for valid FITS HDUs. Most FITS writing applications already know how to insert structural keywords into FITS headers.
  * Keyword records listed in this chapter are arranged alphabetically by name for ease of reference; contributor teams may choose the order of most keywords.
  * Deprecated FITS keywords (such as `EPOCH`)  _**must not**_ be used.

[Common Metadata]

* Note that the lists in this chapter do not include the [basic structural FITS keywords](https://www.aanda.org/index.php?option=com_article&access=standard&Itemid=129&url=/articles/aa/full_html/2010/16/aa15362-10/T30.html) (`SIMPLE`, `NAXIS`, etc.) that are required for valid FITS HDUs. Most FITS writing applications already know how to insert structural keywords into FITS headers.
  * Keyword records listed in this chapter are arranged alphabetically by name for ease of reference; contributor teams may choose the order of most keywords.
  * Deprecated FITS keywords (such as `EPOCH`)  _**must not**_ be used.

title: Common Metadata
text:
* Deprecated FITS keywords (such as `EPOCH`)  _**must not**_ be used.
  * The [FITS Standard](https://fits.gsfc.nasa.gov/fits_standard.html) allows for continued-string (or long-string) keyword records. These are string-valued keywords, with values that are too long to be defined within the character limitation of a single keyword record. This feature must not be used with any of the keywords designated as mandatory or reserved in the FITS Standard, but otherwise may be used without restriction in HLSP science files.
